copy.keyTakeaway: Pirates and Guardians both won big today. Tech stocks took a hit across the board, led by NVDA and MSFT down 3-plus percent. Tonight's NBA Finals tip-off and the Monaco Grand Prix this weekend frame the next 72 hours.

todaysHits.sports: Pirates rout Astros, Guardians embarrass Yankees at home.
todaysHits.markets: Tech selloff continues: SPY -0.7%, NVDA -3.6%, MSFT -3.2%.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial Tournament early.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco Grand Prix rolls this weekend.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup kicks off June 11. Zero matches played yet.
todaysHits.culture: PlayStation State of Play reveals new games and trailers.

lead.headline: Guardians demolished the Yankees in the Bronx
lead.whatHappened: Cleveland rolled into New York and beat the Yankees 9-4, looking completely in control the whole way. The Guardians' offense was relentless while the Yankees' lineup couldn't generate anything resembling a threat.
lead.whyBullet1: The Yankees are supposed to be built for October, and getting punched like this in June tells you their lineup has real holes right now.
lead.whyBullet2: Cleveland keeps proving they're the team to watch in the AL East—they're not a flash in the pan.
lead.whatToSay: Yeah, the Yankees got outplayed at home. This is the kind of loss that sticks with you.

sportsOther: Pirates went into Houston and just took what they wanted.
Guardians walked into the Bronx and made the Yankees look ordinary.
Brewers handled the Giants like it was batting practice.

culture.topic: PlayStation State of Play drops new trailers
culture.whatHappened: Sony held its June 2026 State of Play presentation with fresh game announcements and footage.
culture.whyItMatters: If you own a PS5 or care what's coming next, this is where Sony shows its hand before the rest of the year.
culture.whatToSay: Did you catch the State of Play? Anything in there actually worth a preorder?

culture.topic: Steelers lock up edge rusher Nick Herbig
culture.whatHappened: Pittsburgh signed Nick Herbig to a long-term extension after he emerged as a pass rush threat.
culture.whyItMatters: The Steelers are betting on Herbig to anchor their defense for years. That's real money and real conviction.
culture.whatToSay: Steelers finally got their guy locked in before he could walk.

culture.whatHappened: Seth Rogen plays a film studio exec who genuinely loves movies but runs a company that exists to make sequels and IP.
